title: 261. Rapportnostalgi i Aktiepodden
summary_md:
# 261. Rapportnostalgi i Aktiepodden

**Feed**: aktiepodden  
**Published**: 2026-05-08  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

Episode 261 of Aktiepodden reviews the Q1 2026 reporting season with a nostalgic, mixed tone: macro tailwinds (very low Swedish inflation, the lowest core reading in ~30 years, and rising PMIs in Sweden and Europe) plus a fresh US–Iran ceasefire are fuelling a risk-on rally, while earnings were judged only 'quarter-full' — strong for defensives (banks, healthcare) and cyclicals/industrials but weak for consumer names. The hosts favour small caps over fully-valued large caps once geopolitical risk fades, highlight bombed-out turnaround stocks rallying on modest reports, the Silex (MEMS semiconductor) IPO from Bure amid a soaring SOX index, and an unusual cross-sector 'weather' theme dragging Q1 numbers.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 NOVO-B — Novo Nordisk | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

First earnings beat after a brutal negative cycle; oral weight-loss tablet strong, cost savings biting, 2026 guidance raised on a low baseline.

#### Reasoning

After repeated guidance cuts under the new CEO crushed expectations, Novo finally beat. Oral weight-loss (tablet) sales are strong, savings are landing, and management voices high confidence in its combination preparation. Low expectations plus improving execution set up a cycle of gradual upside surprises.

#### Sell Triggers

- if oral/tablet sales momentum stalls
- if guidance is cut again
- if generic/compounding competition intensifies
- if pivotal study data disappoints

> *"Man höjde väl prognosintervallet för 2026 också i år. Så är det."*

---

### 🟢 ATT — Attendo | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Quality leader among care operators, up ~57% in a year; Finnish operations turned, margin up to 10.1% from 8%, structural elderly-care tailwind.

#### Reasoning

Attendo is seen as the most robust care operator after a tough patch. The previously loss-making Finnish business improved markedly, occupancy is lifting and margins expanded to 10.1%. Low organic growth (0.4%) is fine as this is a profitability story, with a long-term structural tailwind from an ageing population.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Finnish operations relapse
- if occupancy/beläggningsgrad falls
- if election-year 'profits in welfare' politics turns hostile to private care
- if margins contract

> *"den finska verksamheten går väsentligt bättre... rörelsemarginal på 10,1% mot tidigare 8%"*

---

### 🟡 AMBEA — Ambea | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Care operator that has run strongly on right-sizing and rising occupancy; structural winner but unclear how much upside remains.

#### Reasoning

Ambea has performed very well in recent years by right-sizing and lifting occupancy rates. Hosts like the care sector long-term as an ageing-population winner, but flag uncertainty over how much further margin/occupancy improvement is left after the strong run.

#### Sell Triggers

- if occupancy stops improving
- if welfare-profit politics turn hostile
- if cost inflation outpaces pricing

> *"man verkar ha right-sized sig och lyckas nu få upp beläggningsgraden"*

---

### 🟢 THULE — Thule | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Strong report beating expectations with ~4% organic growth; new child/pet product areas up +10%, despite the share falling on the day.

#### Reasoning

Thule guided for decent organic growth, especially in Europe, and delivered better than expected at ~4% organic, with new growth areas (child and pet products) up +10%. The negative share reaction despite a strong print suggests the operational thesis is intact even if sentiment lagged.

#### Sell Triggers

- if organic growth decelerates below guidance
- if European demand weakens
- if new growth categories stop outgrowing the core

> *"organisk tillväxt på ungefär 4%... produkter mot barn och djur som växer plus 10%"*

---

### 🟢 DOM — Dometic | **BUY** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Bombed-out name with decent profitability but collapsed growth; first spring signs in Q1 drove a double-digit share jump, host now positive.

#### Reasoning

Dometic has been a problem child — respectable profitability but imploded growth. Q1 showed early signs of a turn and the stock rose double digits on the report. The host explicitly now views it positively as part of the rotation into beaten-down recovery stories.

#### Sell Triggers

- if growth re-implodes in Q2
- if the green shoots prove one-off
- if consumer/RV demand deteriorates

> *"Nu såg man lite vårt tecken i Q1 och aktien skenade steg två siffrigt på rapport"*

---

### 🟠 HUSQ-B — Husqvarna | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Grouped with the bombed-out turnaround names rallying on improving reports.

#### Reasoning

Husqvarna is mentioned alongside Dometic and Vitrolife as one of the extremely beaten-down stocks catching 'morning air' on modest improvement. The directional read is cautiously constructive but lighter on specifics than the lead turnaround names.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the recovery stalls
- if cyclical/consumer demand weakens
- if margins disappoint

> *"Husqvarna höll också lite grann i det där"*

---

### 🟠 VIT-B — Vitrolife | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

Posted positive organic growth for the first time in a while and was clearly rewarded; extremely bombed-out stock seeing first morning air.

#### Reasoning

Vitrolife delivered positive organic growth for the first time in a while, which the market rewarded distinctly. As one of the most beaten-down names (and a Bure holding), it fits the recovery theme, but the host stops short of a firm conviction call.

#### Sell Triggers

- if organic growth turns negative again
- if IVF demand softens
- if the rebound proves temporary

> *"ett bolag som hade lite positiv organisk tillväxt för första gången på ett tag och det belönade sig tydligt"*

---

### 🟡 ERIC-B — Ericsson | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Delivered a very good report in a run of decent telecom prints.

#### Reasoning

Ericsson is called out as a very good report and part of a string of decent telecom results, signalling improving operational momentum. The view is constructive though framed as an observation rather than an explicit buy thesis.

#### Sell Triggers

- if telecom capex weakens
- if order momentum reverses
- if margins disappoint next quarter

> *"Ericsson var ju en väldigt bra rapport"*

---

### 🟡 ABB — ABB | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Fantastic report with a second straight quarter of incredible order intake driven by the data-center/grid super-cycle.

#### Reasoning

ABB posted a fantastic report with two consecutive quarters of exceptional order intake, tied to the still-strong data-center and grid super-cycle. Hosts cite it as evidence large caps aren't done, with structural demand supporting continued strength.

#### Sell Triggers

- if order intake normalises
- if data-center/grid capex slows
- if valuation outruns fundamentals

> *"Andra kvartalet i rad med helt otrolig orderingång"*

---

### 🟡 MYCR — Mycronic | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Latest report came in very strong, rescuing part of Bure's struggling portfolio.

#### Reasoning

Mycronic's most recent report was very strong, lifting Bure's otherwise tough year. As a high-tech, research-intensive name it benefits from the broader semiconductor strength theme running through the episode.

#### Sell Triggers

- if order intake weakens
- if the semiconductor cycle rolls over
- if a strong quarter proves a one-off

> *"Nu kom Micronic senaste rapport här var väldigt bra, så det räddade ju upp lite grann"*

---

### 🟡 BURE — Bure | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 50%

High-beta, concentrated tech-heavy investment company; rough last year but Mycronic's report and the Silex listing provide redemption.

#### Reasoning

Bure runs a concentrated, high-tech, high-beta portfolio (Micronic, Vitrolife, Ubico) that struggled badly over the past year. A strong Mycronic print and the well-received Silex IPO give it a needed visible success and brand boost. Constructive but tempered by the inherent concentration risk.

#### Sell Triggers

- if portfolio holdings re-derate
- if the Silex listing underperforms
- if the tech/semiconductor cycle turns

> *"jag tror Bure behövde det här att visa en tydlig framgång"*

---

### 🟡 SECU-B — Securitas | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 45%

Tech/installation business (Stanley Security) hit by snowstorms and absence-driven costs; hosts will scrutinise whether deferred installs land in Q2.

#### Reasoning

Securitas blamed weak Q1 tech installations on US snowstorms plus higher costs from staff absence. Hosts (who own it in their fund) stress the need to verify in Q2 that the deferred installations actually materialise — echoing the Ubico cautionary tale where 'timing' excuses preceded a downward spiral.

#### Sell Triggers

- if deferred Q1 installations fail to appear in Q2
- if margins stay pressured by cost inflation
- if demand weakens beyond weather effects

> *"deras teknikprylar där som de är ute och installerar. Det var också väldigt svårt att göra det under snöstormarna"*

---

### 🟠 TD — Tobii Dynavox | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: ST  
**Confidence**: 50%

Growth well below expectations on US snowstorms blocking field sales; CEO says April is strong and Q2 will recover lost sales.

#### Reasoning

Dynavox missed growth expectations, attributed to snowstorms preventing field sales reps from reaching customers. Management was explicit that April looks strong and Q2 will recoup deferred sales. Hosts want to hold them accountable to that recovery rather than take the timing excuse at face value.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Q2 fails to recover the deferred Q1 sales
- if the weather excuse masks underlying demand weakness
- if April momentum does not persist

> *"vdn var väldigt tydliga med att säga att april ser ganska starkt ut... vi kommer ta igen en del av försäljningen under Q2"*

---

### 🟡 HTRO — Hexatronic | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Mixed | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Decent overall report but Fiber Solutions very weak on poor weather.

#### Reasoning

Hexatronic delivered a decent report overall, though its Fiber Solutions segment was very weak, again attributed to bad weather. The directional read is neutral-to-constructive pending whether the weather drag reverses.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Fiber Solutions weakness persists beyond weather
- if order intake softens
- if margins disappoint

> *"Fibersolution går väldigt svagt på grund av att det har varit så pass dåligt väder"*

---

### 🟠 INTC — Intel | **WATCH** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 45%

Former blue-chip that went stale as Nvidia led; now running strongly again and back on track amid a booming chip sector.

#### Reasoning

Intel, long a premium-brand bellwether, lost the lead to Nvidia but is now performing strongly again, reinforcing the broad semiconductor uptrend (SOX near 26-year highs). Mentioned as a positive sector signal rather than a specific Swedish-equity recommendation.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the semiconductor rally reverses
- if Intel fails to regain competitiveness vs Nvidia
- if the SOX index rolls over

> *"Nu har Intel börjat gå jättestarkt igen. Så man ser att även de är tillbaka på banan"*

---
